timeline_html:
Summary. Lay’s hair changes have been ongoing for well over a year, gradually improving over recent months. The history points to long‑term stress as the main backdrop, with some hormonal factors possibly adding sensitivity.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Jun 2025)

Timeline forecast. Stress‑related shedding often builds gradually and can persist while the pressure continues. Based on the typical lag pattern, your shedding likely peaked around late 2025 and began easing by spring 2026. Continued stress management should allow fuller stabilization through mid‑2026, with stronger baby hairs following shortly after. Dominant driver: chronic stress, because it has been continuous and overlaps exactly with your observed shedding period, while other triggers are absent.

Where you are now. You’re in the early regrowth phase—shedding has improved and baby hairs are visible, showing follicles are active again. Keep supporting your recovery and give your scalp time to thicken gradually. 🌱
